steps.title: Fit the table first
steps.content: The table gives the points $(-2,19)$, $(0,31)$, and $(2,43)$. Enter those in Desmos and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$ to get the line quickly.

steps.title: Connect slope to A over B
steps.content: Desmos gives $m=6$, so the line has slope $6$. For an equation in the form $Ax+By=C$, solving for $y$ gives $$y=-\frac{A}{B}x+\frac{C}{B}$$ Therefore the slope is $-\frac{A}{B}$.

steps.title: Answer the question
steps.content: Set $-\frac{A}{B}=6$. Multiplying by $-1$ gives $\frac{A}{B}=-6$. The correct free-response answer is -6.
